fields.name: productionDomain
fields.whyRequired: All HTTPS bindings, certificate SANs, and the Entra OIDC redirect URI are scoped to this domain.
fields.expectedSuccessfulOutput: Domain resolves to the production host with no NXDOMAIN.

fields.name: caCertificateThumbprint
fields.realProvider: Operator enterprise PKI / internal certificate authority
fields.whyRequired: The server certificate must chain to an enterprise CA that domain-joined clients already trust; the thumbprint pins the exact leaf certificate to the IIS binding.
fields.expectedSuccessfulOutput: Exactly one certificate returned, HasPrivateKey = True, NotAfter in the future, issuer = enterprise CA.

fields.name: subjectAlternativeNames
fields.whyRequired: Modern TLS clients validate the hostname against the SAN, not the CN; every name the app is reached by must be present.
fields.expectedSuccessfulOutput: SAN list contains every production hostname; no certificate-name-mismatch warning when browsing https://laf.example.local.

fields.name: certificateExpiry
fields.whyRequired: Expiry must be tracked so a renewal job replaces the certificate before clients see a trust failure.
fields.expectedSuccessfulOutput: NotAfter is in the future and a renewal reminder exists at least 30 days prior.

fields.name: developmentFallbackCertificate
fields.exampleValue: CN=localhost (local test only — self-signed dev certificate, NOT for production)
fields.whyRequired: Documents that local development uses a self-signed CN=localhost certificate which must never be promoted to production.
fields.expectedSuccessfulOutput: A valid HTTPS development certificate is present on the dev machine; this certificate is absent from production.
